perturbation.original_text: Each party agrees that money damages will not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by it or its Representatives, and that the other party is entitled specific performance and injunctive relief as remedies for any such breach. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be exclusive remedies for a breach of this Agreement but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party agrees that money damages will be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by it or its Representatives, and that the other party is entitled specific performance and injunctive relief as remedies for any such breach. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be exclusive remedies for a breach of this Agreement but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'will not be a sufficient remedy' to 'will be a sufficient remedy' introduces a direct contradiction within Section 12. The original states that money damages are *insufficient*, implying other remedies are necessary. The modified text states money damages *are* sufficient, yet it also still grants specific performance and injunctive relief. This creates confusion about whether monetary compensation fully resolves a breach or if further legal actions are still required and adds in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12. Relief. Each party agrees that money damages will be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by it or its Representatives, and that the other party is entitled specific performance and injunctive relief as remedies for any such breach. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be exclusive remedies for a breach of this Agreement but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that money damages will *not* be a sufficient remedy, implying that other remedies are necessary. The changed text states that money damages *will* be a sufficient remedy, yet still grants specific performance and injunctive relief. This creates a contradiction regarding whether monetary compensation fully resolves a breach or if further legal actions are still required. Since Section 12 is both the Changed Text and the Contradicted Text, the change introduces a direct contradiction within the same section, fitting the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' as the clause now has conflicting implications in the same location.

perturbation.original_text: 8. Definitive Agreements. Unless and until a final definitive agreement, regarding the Transaction has been executed, no contract or agreement with respect to the Transaction shall be deemed to exist between the parties and neither party will be under any legal obligation whatsoever with respect to the Transaction by virtue of this Agreement except for the matters specifically agreed to herein. The term “definitive agreement” does not include a term sheet or any other preliminary written agreement. Each party reserves the right, in its sole discretion, to provide or not provide Evaluation Material under this Agreement, to reject any and all proposals with regard to the Transaction and to terminate discussions and negotiations at any time.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Definitive Agreements. Unless and until a final definitive agreement, regarding the Transaction has been executed, a term sheet will be considered a binding contract and agreement with respect to the Transaction shall be deemed to exist between the parties and neither party will be under any legal obligation whatsoever with respect to the Transaction by virtue of this Agreement except for the matters specifically agreed to herein. Each party reserves the right, in its sole discretion, to provide or not provide Evaluation Material under this Agreement, to reject any and all proposals with regard to the Transaction and to terminate discussions and negotiations at any time.
perturbation.explanation: Adding 'a term sheet will be considered a binding contract' introduces a contradiction within Section 8. The original states that 'no contract or agreement with respect to the Transaction shall be deemed to exist' until a final definitive agreement. However, modifying it to include that 'a term sheet will be considered a binding contract' implies there is a binding agreement *before* a definitive agreement is reached, which contradicts the rest of the original sentence.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. Definitive Agreements. Unless and until a final definitive agreement, regarding the Transaction has been executed, a term sheet will be considered a binding contract and agreement with respect to the Transaction shall be deemed to exist between the parties and neither party will be under any legal obligation whatsoever with respect to the Transaction by virtue of this Agreement except for the matters specifically agreed to herein. Each party reserves the right, in its sole discretion, to provide or not provide Evaluation Material under this Agreement, to reject any and all proposals with regard to the Transaction and to terminate discussions and negotiations at any time.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that 'a term sheet will be considered a binding contract', which directly contradicts the initial part of the same sentence stating that 'no contract or agreement with respect to the Transaction shall be deemed to exist' until a final definitive agreement is executed. This creates a clear and direct conflict within Section 8, as the term sheet is now defined as a binding contract, going against the original intent of only definitive agreements being binding.

perturbation.original_text: Each party agrees to be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party agrees to not be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives.
perturbation.explanation: By negating the original text from 'Each party agrees to be responsible' to 'Each party agrees to not be responsible' introduce a contradiction within Section 10. It introduces an in-text contradiction in the same line as the new negation, adding confusion and uncertainty and thus adding a flaw to it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. Miscellaneous. Each party agrees to not be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives. In case any provision of this Agreement shall be. invalid, illegal or unenforceable, the validity, legality and enforceability of the remaining provisions of the Agreement shall not in any way be affected or impaired thereby,
perturbation.justification: The original text states each party is responsible for breaches, while the changed text states each party is not responsible for breaches by its representatives. This directly contradicts the original statement, and also contradicts section 10, which now also states that each party agrees to not be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives. This creates a clear and direct in-text contradiction and creates uncertainty about the obligations and rights of each party, which aligns with the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 11. Governing Law, Jurisdiction. This Agreement and all matters arising out of or relating to this Agreement shall be governed by the procedural and substantive laws of the state of California and shall be deemed executed in Redwood City, California. Any legal action or proceeding relating to this Agreement shall be instituted exclusively in any state or federal court In San Francisco or San Mateo County, California. Company and Oracle irrevocably and unconditionally agree to submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of, and agree that the venue is proper in, the aforesaid courts in any such legal action or proceeding.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. Governing Law, Jurisdiction. This Agreement and all matters arising out of or relating to this Agreement shall be governed by the procedural and substantive laws of the state of New York and shall be deemed executed in Redwood City, California. Any legal action or proceeding relating to this Agreement shall be instituted exclusively in any state or federal court In San Francisco or San Mateo County, California. Company and Oracle irrevocably and unconditionally agree to submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of, and agree that the venue is proper in, the aforesaid courts in any such legal action or proceeding.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the governing law from California to New York creates a conflict within Section 11. The venue and jurisdiction remain in California courts, but the laws of New York are to be applied. This contradiction raises uncertainty on which state's legal principles will be used in interpreting the contract since the venue is in California, but the governing law is now New York.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11. Governing Law, Jurisdiction. This Agreement and all matters arising out of or relating to this Agreement shall be governed by the procedural and substantive laws of the state of New York and shall be deemed executed in Redwood City, California. Any legal action or proceeding relating to this Agreement shall be instituted exclusively in any state or federal court In San Francisco or San Mateo County, California. Company and Oracle irrevocably and unconditionally agree to submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of, and agree that the venue is proper in, the aforesaid courts in any such legal action or proceeding.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the governing law from California to New York while maintaining the venue in California. This creates a direct conflict within Section 11 because the laws of New York will govern a case tried in California. The explanation clearly states that the contradiction raises uncertainty on which state's legal principles will be used. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, meaning the contradiction exists within the same section. Based on the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction,' placing a clause in multiple locations with conflicting implications causes a contradiction. Although it's in the same location, the implication of having New York law govern a case in California creates a direct contradiction. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.
